summary: Backend-only verification of the 7 Trading Engine Logic Updates on LIVE OKX data. 18/18 pytest cases passed against https://futures-analyzer-4.preview.emergentagent.com. All 9 invariants (grade set, action set, A+ contract, WAIT contract, auto_rr set + rr==auto_rr, entry_quality_score/label banding, level ordering per direction, schema stability, fresh=1 HTTP 200) hold for every setup returned by /api/scan (best_setups + preparing) and /api/setup/{BTC,ETH,SOL} in both cached and fresh modes. Contradiction sweep over 18 combined setups found ZERO self-contradictions. Regression endpoints (/api/market/pairs, /api/goals/summary, /api/watchlist) also pass. Note: the scan API returns `preparing` (not `preparing_setups` as mentioned in the request); tests treat both names as equivalent and use the actual `preparing` array.

3_A+_contract.example: No A+ present in current scan sample (grade_dist_best={A:8}); A+ contract enforced defensively in code and by _assert_setup_invariants when encountered on setup detail as well â€” no violations observed.

4_WAIT_contract.example: All 10 `preparing` WAIT items have gradeâˆˆ{B,C} and confidenceâ‰¤82; BTC/ETH/SOL setup detail also WAIT with grade C/B and confidence â‰¤ 82.

5_auto_rr_and_rr_equal.example: BTCUSDT auto_rr=2.0 risk_reward=2.0; BZUSDT auto_rr=1.5 risk_reward=1.5. All values âˆˆ {1.5,2.0,2.5,3.0}.

6_entry_quality_label_matches_score.example: BZUSDT eqs=16 â†’ 'Wait for Pullback' (matches band <50). All 18 setups have int scoreâˆˆ[0,100] and label matches band.

7_level_ordering_per_direction.example: long BZUSDT: sl<entry<tp1. short setups sl>entry>tp1. Neutral BTCUSDT skipped (no direction enforced).

8_fresh_1_returns_200_bypass.example: Two back-to-back /api/setup/BTCUSDT?timeframe=1h&fresh=1 calls both HTTP 200 in <60s. price1=63199.2, price2=63199.2 (diff=0.00). Cache is technically bypassed (no cache headers, latency similar), but during a quiet minute prices may naturally coincide â€” recommend main agent add a `served_at`/`data_ts` field so fresh vs cached is unambiguously distinguishable.

9_no_500s_no_missing_keys_stable_schema.example: All required keys (symbol, direction, price, entry, stop_loss, take_profit_1/2/3, risk_reward, auto_rr, ai_score, confidence, trade_grade, action, entry_quality_score, entry_quality_label, display_checklist, support, resistance) present on every response.

minor.issue: Response key is `preparing`, but main agent/reviewer spec referred to `preparing_setups`. Naming inconsistency between spec and impl â€” consider aliasing or renaming for clarity.

Cache bypass produces identical price on back-to-back calls during quiet OKX minutes (BTC price1=price2=63199.2). Fresh path returns 200 quickly, but there is no `served_at`/`data_ts`/`cache: MISS` marker in the response body to unambiguously prove cache bypass. Recommend adding a `data_ts` epoch millisecond or `served_from: fresh|cache` field.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add a `data_ts` / `served_from` marker to /api/setup and /api/scan so cache-bypass (fresh=1) is unambiguously observable from the response body.
OPTIONAL: Normalize scan response key naming â€” either rename `preparing` â†’ `preparing_setups` or update the spec (agent_communication in test_result.md still uses the shorter `preparing`).
critical_code_review_comments: Grade/action/checklist wiring is well-anchored (build_setup uses display_checklist as single source of truth). No self-contradictions observed across 18 live setups.
Entry Quality banding is implemented cleanly and matches the (>=85 Excellent, >=70 Good, >=50 Fair, else Wait for Pullback) contract.
auto_rr is always echoed into `risk_reward` â€” no divergence across BTC/ETH/SOL/BZ live samples.

WAIT clamp (confidence â‰¤ 82) held across all 10 preparing items. A+ path could not be exercised in this scan sample because no symbol currently satisfies 6/6 checklist + structure_ok + trend_ok â€” expected behavior of strict grading.
seed_data_creation: None â€” used seeded Bearer token test-token-abc-123 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md for authed spot-checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend 7 trading-engine-logic updates fully verified live on OKX. If frontend UI testing is next: focus on TradeCard rendering (auto_rr pill, entry_quality label with color band) and analyze/[symbol] refresh flow calling fresh=1. Also note grade A+ is currently absent from the live universe, so UI must gracefully render A/B/C without crashes. Scan bucket key is `preparing` not `preparing_setups`.
rca_of_issue: N/A â€” no failures. Only two soft observations: (1) fresh=1 price parity on quiet minutes (need a server-side timestamp marker to prove bypass), (2) `preparing` vs `preparing_setups` naming mismatch between spec doc and implementation.
